channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Oh Nice, Spyro Is Back And Looks Great
item.content:encoded:
Today during Xbox’s big summer showcase, Activision and Microsoft announced the next entry in the long-running and popular Spyro franchise. It’s called Spyro: A Realm Beyond, and it’s out next year on all platforms.

Here’s the trailer, which shows off some early gameplay.

Xbox calls this a “brand-new adventure” that is being developed by Toys for Bob.  Here’s more info from Xbox’s official YouTube description for the trailer:

Feel the freedom of flight as you soar above vibrant landscapes, weave between treetops, dive off massive landmarks and wing your way through this new realm in Spyro ’s boldest evolution yet.

There have been rumors and leaks about a new Spyro game for a bit now, with Toys for Bob attached as the developer. The studio worked on the well-received Spyro remastered trilogy as well as Crash Bandicoot 4, and before all that, the Skylanders series, which was a spin-off of Spyro, so the folks there have a lot of experience with everyone’s favorite purple dragon. It’s nice to see Xbox and Activision investing in big, colorful platformers.

Spyro: A Realm Beyond is set to launch on Xbox, PS5, Switch 2, and PC in 2027.



https://kotaku.com/oh-nice-spyro-is-back-and-looks-great-2000703677
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Hellblade Game Simply Titled Senua And Will Be Out Next Year
item.content:encoded:
Ninja Theory has announced the next game in the Hellblade series, Senua. Coming sometime next year, it drops the “Hellblade” name in favor of keeping it simple.

According to an interview with the game’s developers, the new title format alludes to a new direction for the series. Senua will feature more in-depth combat and puzzle solving, turning the game into a more action-packed experience. Players will have options on how they engage with enemies, and Senua will also focus on exploration, offering rewards to players who traverse off the beaten path. Hellblade games are also an immersive experience known for featuring incredible mocap performances and audio design.

Senua’s last adventure, Hellblade II, launched in 2024 and according to the developers, Senua will be twice as big, making the 2027 release date a quick turnaround for a larger and more technically evolved game.



https://kotaku.com/new-hellblade-game-simply-titled-senua-and-will-be-out-next-year-2000703672
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: There’s A Translucent Green Xbox Series X That Looks So Awesome I Don’t Want To Know The Price
item.content:encoded:
It’s been almost 25 years since the original Xbox came out and Microsoft is celebrating in part with a new limited-edition Xbox Series X that is translucent green and looks amazing. Thankfully for my wallet, the company is hiding the price tag at the moment.

Revealed during the 2026 Xbox summer showcase on Sunday, the “XBOX” Series X25 Limited Edition console comes with a 1TB SSD encased in beautiful green see-thru plastic. That’s essentially all the info we have about it but we did get this nostalgia-bomb of an ad to go with it:

Now this is an XBOX! pic.twitter.com/7FeE9n7u8h

— ASHA (@asha_shar) June 7, 2026

The console will also come out alongside a translucent OG Green Xbox Series X/S controller with an Xbox insignia that lights up green and elements like the bumpers that call back to the original “Duke” gamepad for Microsoft’s first console. That will be sold separately when both limited-edition devices launch sometime ahead of the holiday season in November.

How much will all of this cost? Microsoft isn’t saying yet. “We’ll have more to share on pricing and preorder availability at select retailers soon,” the company wrote in a blog post. Thanks, generative AI!

The 1TB Xbox Series X currently retails for $650. Hopefully, the limited edition 25-year anniversary model is still below $700 by the time it comes out.



https://kotaku.com/theres-a-translucent-green-xbox-series-x-that-looks-so-awesome-i-dont-want-to-know-the-price-2000703669
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
